etymology_and_history.root_language: Language**: From "self" + "satisfy" + "-ed" from Latin "satisfacere" (to do enough for oneself)
etymology_and_history.original_meaning: Meaning**: Do enough oneself; experiencing complacent smug and self-satisfied complacent supported by growth awareness
etymology_and_history.semantic_evolution: Evolution**: Do enough → complacent smug → contemporary self-satisfied complacent and self-satisfied smug that benefits from humility growth
etymology_and_history.cultural_shifts: Shifts**: Do enough → complacent smug → self-satisfied complacent → humility understanding

contemporary_usage.primary_definition: Definition**: Having self-satisfied complacent and self-satisfied smug; experiencing complacent smug and self-satisfied self-satisfaction calling for growth humility
contemporary_usage.register: Negative mild; indicates complacency calling for growth support and humility development
